description: Colombian far-right paramilitary and drug trafficking group active from 1997 to 2006. Formed through a merger of local right-wing militias to combat left-wing insurgents. Heavily financed through drug trade and support from landowners and companies. Responsible for numerous attacks on civilians and designated as a terrorist organization by several countries. Demobilized by 2006, but successor groups continue.

country_links.evidence_quote: The AUC was designated as a terrorist organization by many countries and organizations, including the United States , Canada and the European Union
country_links.context: The AUC was designated as a terrorist organization by Canada.

country_links.evidence_quote: Israeli trainers arrived in Colombia in 1987 to train him and other paramilitaries who would later make up the AUC. Fifty of the paramilitaries' 'best' students were then sent on scholarships to Israel for further training
country_links.context: Israeli trainers arrived in Colombia to train AUC paramilitaries, and some AUC members were sent to Israel for further training.

country_links.evidence_quote: The AUC was designated as a terrorist organization by many countries and organizations, including the United States
country_links.context: The AUC was designated as a terrorist organization by the United States, and its leadership was extradited there.

country_links.evidence_quote: Venezuelan authorities arrested at least 100 individuals that they accused of being Colombian paramilitaries and of scheming... to begin a series of scheduled attacks... aiming at the overthrow of President Hugo Chávez
country_links.context: Venezuelan authorities arrested individuals accused of being Colombian paramilitaries and of scheming to overthrow President Chávez.
